symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable non-bilious vomiting with distension (gastroparesis flare)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intractable non-bilious vomiting with distension on a known hEDS/gastroparesis background fits a visceral flare.
Non-bilious, non-faeculent vomiting with passage of flatus is against complete obstruction, though it needs assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic hypotension/tachycardia, dry mucosa, unable to keep fluids down

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural hypotension (85/50 standing), tachycardia and dry mucosa with inability to rehydrate orally indicate significant volume depletion approaching shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinary retention (unable to empty; palpable bladder)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to empty with a palpable bladder indicates retention needing decompression.

symptoms.symptom_name: No faeculent/bilious vomiting, flatus passed, no peritonism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Passage of flatus, non-faeculent vomiting and a soft abdomen argue against a surgical abdomen, though it must be re-examined.

symptoms.symptom_name: No DKA features (reported normal glucose, no Kussmaul breathing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal glucose and absence of deep laboured breathing argue against DKA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended tympanic abdomen with sluggish sounds, no peritonism; palpable bladder; hEDS hyperextensibility (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distension with hypomotility and a palpable retained bladder fits a visceral/autonomic flare, with dehydration.
No peritonism argues against peritonitis, but re-examination is needed.
hEDS is the substrate for gastroparesis, dysautonomia and pelvic-floor dysfunction.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: hEDS visceral (gastroparesis) flare with significant dehydration and urinary retention
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Intractable vomiting with orthostatic hypotension approaching shock, inability to rehydrate orally, and urinary retention (palpable bladder), on a known hEDS background, needs urgent in-person/ED assessment for IV fluids, IV antiemesis, bladder decompression and to exclude a surgical abdomen — not remote antiemetics or small sips.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person/ED abdominal and bladder assessment with postural vitals
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess dehydration/shock, exclude a surgical abdomen, and confirm/relieve urinary retention; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: IV rehydration, IV antiemesis, and bladder catheterisation; bloods (U&E, glucose/ketones, FBC)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To correct volume depletion, control vomiting, decompress the bladder, and check electrolytes/renal function.
If assessment raises concern for mechanical obstruction.
